used:
begin at once to live, and count each separate day as a separate life.||i knew a man who never missed a day.
every morning, same routine, same excuse.

he said, "tomorrow's a new start."
i watched him put off the things he loved.

each day stacked like old newspapers.
unread, forgotten, lifeless by evening.

we live as if today’s a rehearsal.
but every sunrise is a full life.

you can’t borrow tomorrow’s breath.
each day counts as its own life.

he died still waiting to live.
that’s the only freedom any of us ever had.

it never ceases to amaze me: we all love ourselves more than other people, but care more about their opinion than our own.||most people i knew
worried more about what others thought.
not what they really felt inside.

i spent years watching young folks
bend themselves to fit the crowd.
it was hard for me, too.

we love ourselves more than anyone.
yet, we listen to every whisper outside.
that’s where we get stuck.

i learned to ask myself one thing.
whose opinion will matter at the end?
not many answers came from outside.

you carry your own company always.
that’s the hardest and loneliest truth.

look well into thyself; there is a source of strength which will always spring up if thou wilt always look.||i saw a man come back to school years later.
he carried a suitcase full of regrets.

he told me he never stopped looking inside himself.
but somehow, he stopped seeing his own strength.

there’s a well inside each of us.
it’s quiet and steady if you pay attention.

most people look everywhere else first.
they forget the power waiting in their own hands.

i found that well when i was just tired.
it kept me standing when everything else failed.

strength isn’t always loud.
sometimes it’s the stillness that holds you up.

never let the future disturb you. you will meet it, if you have to, with the same weapons of reason which today arm you against the present.||i saw a boy once, worried about tomorrow.
he gripped his books tighter than the test.

tomorrow was a distant storm to him.
he thought the panic helped him prepare.

but the mind works with what it has now.
the same reason that guards you today
will meet the next day’s trouble too.

i had to remind myself of that many times.
fear of the future steals the moment you’re in.

your tools are here, in this very hour.
no need to borrow worry from a day not born.

one day, the future arrives quietly.
and all you ever had was right here.

that’s the only armor anyone ever needs.

the first rule is to keep an untroubled spirit. the second is to look things in the face and know them for what they are.||i saw a teacher once get upset every day.
not at students, but at the small details.

he never stopped to breathe it all in.
never learned to face things clearly.

it took me years to understand that.
you carry too much when your spirit’s troubled.

looking at trouble straight, knowing what it is,
makes it less heavy somehow.

peace isn’t about ignoring what’s wrong.
it’s about not letting it fill your whole mind.

that quiet is the place where real strength hides.

you always own the option of having no opinion. there is never any need to get worked up or to trouble your soul about things you can't control.||i saw grown men shout at small things.
things that never meant much to begin with.

they carried that anger like a coat.
heavy. hot. always uncomfortable.

i learned you don’t have to hold an opinion.
sometimes the wisest answer is none at all.

let the noise pass through.
it’s not your burden to carry.

the hardest part is letting go.
but peace starts with silence.

you can choose to trouble your soul.
or you can choose to be free.
